seo.title: Building Code Generation with Instructor + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Instructor + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Instructor simplifies structured code generation by patching LLM clients to return validated Pydantic models, enabling reliable extraction of code snippets, tests, and metadata from LLM responses. When building code generation agents that iterate through sandbox execution and refinement, guardrails are essential to prevent syntax errors, security vulnerabilities, and credential leaks. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure generated code meets safety and functional requirements before execution.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Instructor
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture starts with an Instructor-patched LLM client generating structured outputs like code, test cases, and execution plans as Pydantic models. The agent receives a task, calls the LLM to produce code with accompanying tests, then executes it in a sandboxed environment (e.g., Docker or E2B). Results from execution feed back into the LLM for refinement iterations until tests pass or limits are hit.

Instructor ensures consistent extraction of nested fields like code language, dependencies, and test assertions, while Vex verifies runtime properties like syntax validity and safety. This closed-loop system handles complex tasks like algorithm implementation or data processing pipelines.

Key components include the Instructor client wrapper, sandbox executor, test validator, and Vex verification layer that gates execution.

risks.name: Malicious code injection
risks.description: LLM generates code with system calls, file writes, or network requests that escape sandbox
risks.mitigation: Vex static analysis + sandbox syscall filtering

risks.name: Credential leakage in code
risks.description: Instructor extracts code containing hardcoded API keys or secrets from training data
risks.mitigation: Vex regex pattern matching + secret scanning

risks.name: Unsafe dependency installation
risks.description: Generated requirements.txt includes malicious packages or version conflicts

risks.name: Syntax errors halting execution
risks.description: Partial code extraction fails syntax checks, breaking iteration loop
risks.mitigation: Vex AST parsing + syntax validation before sandbox

implementation.title: Instructor Pydantic Model for Code Generation
implementation.description: Define structured output model with code, tests, and metadata for reliable extraction
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List
import openai

class CodeGenerationResponse(BaseModel):
    language: str = Field(..., description="Programming language")
    code: str = Field(..., description="Complete executable code")
    tests: List[str] = Field(..., description="Pytest test functions")
    dependencies: List[str] = Field(default=[], description="Required packages")
    description: str = Field(..., max_length=200)

client = from_openai(openai.OpenAI())

response = client.messages.create(
    model="gpt-4o",
    response_model=CodeGenerationResponse,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write Python quicksort with tests"}]
)
print(response.code)

implementation.title: Sandbox Execution with Test Validation
implementation.description: Execute generated code in isolated environment and validate tests
implementation.code:
import subprocess
import tempfile
import os

def execute_sandboxed(code: str, tests: List[str], deps: List[str]):
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        # Install dependencies
        if deps:
            subprocess.run(["pip", "install", *deps], cwd=tmpdir, check=True)
        
        # Write code and tests
        with open(os.path.join(tmpdir, "solution.py"), "w") as f:
            f.write(code)
        with open(os.path.join(tmpdir, "test_solution.py"), "w") as f:
            f.write("import pytest\n" + "\n".join(tests))
        
        # Run tests
        result = subprocess.run([
            "pytest", "test_solution.py", "-v"
        ], cwd=tmpdir, capture_output=True, text=True)
        
        return {
            "passed": result.returncode == 0,
            "output": result.stdout,
            "error": result.stderr
        }

vexIntegration.title: Vex Safety Verification Before Execution
vexIntegration.description: Wrap Instructor response with Vex to verify code safety before sandbox

vexIntegration.title: Syntax + Dependency Validation
vexIntegration.description: Vex checks syntax validity and dependency safety before installation
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import verify
import ast

@verify(guards=[
    "python_syntax_valid",
    "no_malicious_packages",
    "dependency_allowlist:requests,numpy,pandas"
])
def validate_and_execute(response: CodeGenerationResponse):
    # Vex validates syntax + deps
    result = execute_sandboxed(
        response.code, 
        response.tests, 
        response.dependencies
    )
    return result

# Integrates seamlessly with Instructor
response = client.messages.create(...)
result = validate_and_execute(response)
